entries.note: The CrashRecoveryBanner component, its route integration, and three foundational smoke tests were already present in the worktree (from a prior dashboard-features session). This session completed the remaining acceptance-criteria coverage: (1) added workflowUpdateFrame() to e2e/helpers.ts so tests can send workflow.update frames from the server side; (2) added AC-3/RC-3 test verifying that clicking 'Acknowledge & Resume' transitions the button to a spinner and that the outgoing control frame carries the correct resumeMethod from recoveryState (not hardcoded); (3) added AC-5 test verifying the banner unmounts when a workflow.update frame with recoveryState: null arrives. All 22 workflow-detail smoke tests pass. The component itself was not modified — it already satisfied all ACs and RCs.
known_risks: optimistic spinner on resume has no timeout — if the server never sends workflow.update the button stays disabled forever (by design for a localhost app, but worth noting)

the control frame capture in the AC-3/RC-3 test asserts on sentMessages which accumulates all frames; if subscribe retries send extra frames the assertion is still safe because it checks the first control frame only
